IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Please note that this shutdown was previously scheduled for the 20th May 2026 but was cancelled on the day. Some trees have grown too close to overhead power lines in your area. If left unmanaged, branches may come into contact with the lines, which could lead to unexpected interruptions to your electricity supply. To help prevent this, a tree surgeon will cut back the trees. The work has been requested by a third party, and their appointed tree surgeon will carry it out. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut back trees near the overhead power lines. Doing this helps reduce the risk of you experiencing unexpected power cuts in the future and maintains a safe and reliable electricity supply as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Folder Lane. Multi-way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 300 metres across 2 sections of the network. Due to the scale of the work, the power interruption is approximately 5 hours. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Boxhill Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area. As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 15 electricity poles. These poles play an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. We understand that power interruptions are inconvenient and we will do everything we can to keep disruption to a minimum. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place near Millers Green Road and Shellow Road.

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 08:11 - Our engineers have restored power to all 3477 properties in the ME8 0, ME8 8, ME9 7,  ME9 8 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around a faulted piece of overhead network equipment

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the ME8 0, ME8 8, ME9 7, ME9 8 area to investigate the power cut affecting 256 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by David at 07:38 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is faulty overhead electrical equipment that initially interrupted power to 3477 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 256 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 10:30-11:30, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? Steps.Message:
Hi, this is Lisa from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I'm sorry for the power cut today; I know the lights going off can be disruptive.

We know that some customers affected by todays power cut may have had other issues recently, and I appreciate this is frustrating. High voltage cabling can cover a wide area, so sometimes properties very close to you may have had power cuts that you didn't experience.

I know you may not have had any previous power cuts, but if you have, we often get progress updates from our field staff management about investigations into repeat issues, and our Customer Services team will try to contact everyone affected by them to explain what's happening.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Lisa at 20:23 - Our engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is an overhead cable that is on the ground, affecting 1057 properties.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, so it's possible power could come back on at any point. We can't always restore everyone this way. That's why we're also sending staff to the area to check our equipment.

Every fault is different, so our time frames have to be estimated but currently we hope to have power back on between 22:30 and 23:30. We must stress that it may become necessary for this to change, depending on what our engineers find.

We'll keep you updated with our progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Donny at 07:57 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead network fault that initially interrupted power to 3105 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 798 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 11:00-12:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
